Kick off a task in your terminal and pick it up from your phone while you take a walk or join a meeting. Claude keeps running on your machine, and you can control the session from the Claude app or claude.ai/code.","url":"https://www.threads.com/@claudeai/post/DVKDCc6ESAe/video-new-in-claude-code-remote-control-kick-off-a-task-in-your-terminal-and-pick-it"},{"title":"Claude Code Remote Control: Complete Feature Guide","url":"https://www.digitalapplied.com/blog/claude-code-remote-control-feature-guide"},{"title":"Claude Code Remote Control: How to Use Claude Code from Your Phone | Data Science Dojo","url":"https://datasciencedojo.com/blog/claude-code-remote-control/"},{"title":"Claude Code Remote Control: A 3-Minute Overview and When to Actually Use It - SmartScope","url":"https://smartscope.blog/en/generative-ai/claude/claude-code-remote-control/"},{"title":"Claude Code Remote Control: Manage AI Coding Sessions From Any Device | by Flora Nanda | Data Science Collective | Feb, 2026 | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/data-science-collective/claude-code-remote-control-manage-ai-coding-sessions-from-any-device-2480b0907d90"},{"title":"Claude Code Remote Control","url":"https://simonwillison.net/2026/Feb/25/claude-code-remote-control/"}]

Here's a comprehensive overview of the **Claude Code Remote Control** feature based on the latest documentation and sources:

---

## 🖥️ Claude Code Remote Control — Feature Overview

### What Is It?
Remote Control connects claude.ai/code or the Claude app for iOS and Android to a Claude Code session running on your machine. It was introduced in February 2026 as a research preview. It's a synchronization layer, not a cloud migration — your session keeps running on your machine the entire time.

---

### 🔑 Key Differentiator: Local vs. Cloud
The key difference from Claude Code on the web is where the session runs: Remote Control executes on your machine, so your local MCP servers, tools, and project configuration stay available, while Claude Code on the web executes in Anthropic-managed cloud infrastructure.

---

### 🔒 Security Model
Your local Claude Code session makes outbound HTTPS requests only and never opens inbound ports on your machine. When you start Remote Control, it registers with the Anthropic API and polls for work. When you connect from another device, the server routes messages between the web or mobile client and your local session over a streaming connection.

Because every connection is outbound, Remote Control works behind NAT, corporate firewalls, and home routers without any configuration changes — no port forwarding and no VPN required.

The connection uses multiple short-lived credentials, each scoped to a single purpose and expiring independently.

---

### 📋 Requirements
Remote Control requires Claude Code v2.1.51 or later. It is available on Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plans — API keys are not supported. On Team and Enterprise, an admin must first enable the Remote Control toggle in Claude Code admin settings.

---

### ⚡ How to Start a Session
There are two key commands to know: `claude rc` is a terminal command that starts a **new** session and accepts flags like `--sandbox`, while `/rc` is a slash command inside an **existing** session — it carries over conversation history but doesn't accept flag options. Use the former for new tasks, the latter for taking ongoing work mobile.

---

### 📱 Connecting from Another Device
The most reliable connection method is via the session list: open claude.ai/code in any browser or the Claude mobile app, and Remote Control sessions appear with a computer icon and a green status dot. On macOS, pressing the spacebar may display a QR code for quick phone access via the Claude mobile app — this feature does not currently work on Windows.

---

### 🔄 Resilience & Interruptions
If your laptop sleeps or your network drops, the session reconnects automatically when your machine comes back online. Sleep recovery is automatic — closing your laptop lid triggers sleep, and reconnection happens automatically on wake — but OS shutdown or crash kills the session.

---

### 💡 Practical Tips
Key best practices include: giving descriptive task instructions before walking away, setting up MCP servers before starting (you can't add new MCP servers from mobile), and using `/rc` rather than `claude rc` to preserve conversation context.

Also recommended: name your session before going remote by running `/rename` before `/rc`, as Remote Control sessions default to your last message content or "Remote Control session," which makes them hard to identify when juggling multiple sessions.

---

### 📚 Related Documentation
Additional official docs cover: Claude Code on the web, Channels (forwarding Telegram or Discord into a session), Authentication, a full CLI reference including `claude remote-control`, Security, and Data usage.

Claude Code Remote Control Feature Launched, Turning Your Phone into a Computer Terminal Powerhouse","url":"https://www.aibase.com/news/25659"},{"title":"Claude Code Remote Control","url":"https://simonwillison.net/2026/Feb/25/claude-code-remote-control/"}]

Here is a comprehensive summary of the **Claude Code Remote Control** feature based on the latest release notes and sources:

---

## 🚀 Claude Code Remote Control — Feature & Release Notes

### What Is It?
Remote Control is a synchronization layer that bridges local CLI environments with the Claude mobile app and web interface. It's a synchronization layer, not a cloud migration — your session keeps running on your machine the entire time.

### Initial Release
Remote Control is a new feature (launched February 2026, research preview) that bridges your local Claude Code terminal session with claude.ai/code, the Claude iOS app, and the Claude Android app.

To access the feature, users must update to **Claude version 2.1.52** and execute the command `claude remote-control` or use the in-session slash command `/rc`.

### Availability
Currently, Remote Control is available as a Research Preview for subscribers on the **Claude Max tier**. While access for Claude Pro ($20/month) users is expected shortly, the feature remains a high-end tool for power users and is notably absent from Team or Enterprise plans during this initial phase.

### How It Works
The terminal generates a unique session URL and a QR code (toggleable via the spacebar) for your mobile device. Once you open that link on your phone, tablet, or another browser, the two surfaces stay in perfect sync — allowing you to start a task at your desk and continue it from anywhere while maintaining full access to your local filesystem and project configuration.

Your local Claude Code session makes outbound HTTPS requests only — no inbound ports are opened on your machine. When you start Remote Control, the process registers with the Anthropic API and polls for work. When you connect from another device, the server routes messages between the client and your local session over a streaming connection. All traffic flows through the Anthropic API over TLS.

### Key Bug Fixes & Improvements (from Changelog)

- **Several Remote Control issues fixed**: sessions silently dying when the server reaps an idle environment, rapid messages being queued one-at-a-time instead of batched, and stale work items causing redelivery after JWT refresh.
- **Fixed** "media_type: Field required" API error when receiving images via Remote Control.
- **Fixed** Remote Control sessions showing a generic title instead of deriving from the first prompt.
- **Fixed** Android app crash when running local slash commands (`/voice`, `/cost`) in Remote Control sessions.
- **Improved** the Remote Control spawn mode selection prompt with better context.
- **Fixed** bridge sessions failing to recover after extended WebSocket disconnects.

### Known Limitations
- There is a 10-minute network timeout: if your machine can't reach the network for roughly 10 minutes, the session times out. Permission approval is still required even with Remote Control active. It is available on **Pro and Max plans only** — not available on Team or Enterprise plans.
- It appears not to support the `--dangerously-skip-permissions` flag, which means you have to approve every new action it takes.

### Where to Learn More
- Official Changelog: [code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog)
- GitHub Releases: [github.com/anthropics/claude-code/releases](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/releases)
- Official Documentation: [code.claude.com/docs/remote-control](https://code.claude.com/docs/remote-control)

content.text:
以下是关于 Claude Code Remote Control 功能的完整说明：

---

## Claude Code Remote Control 功能介绍

### 引入版本

该功能于 **2026 年 2 月**作为 Research Preview（研究预览版）推出，要求 **Claude Code v2.1.51 或更高版本**。

---

### 这个功能是什么？

Remote Control 是一个同步层，将本地运行的 Claude Code 终端会话与以下设备/界面连接起来：

- `claude.ai/code`（网页端）
- Claude iOS 移动应用
- Claude Android 移动应用

核心理念：**会话始终运行在你的本地机器上**，而非迁移到云端。这意味着你的本地 MCP 服务器、工具配置和项目设置全部保持可用。

---

### 工作原理

1. 在终端执行 `claude rc`（新建会话）或 `/rc`（在现有会话内启动）
2. 你的本地 Claude Code 进程向 Anthropic API 发出**出站 HTTPS 请求**注册会话，不会在本机开放任何入站端口
3. 终端会生成一个会话 URL 和 QR 码（macOS 下按空格键切换显示）
4. 从手机或其他浏览器打开该链接后，Anthropic 服务器在客户端与本地会话之间通过流式连接路由消息
5. 两端保持实时同步，支持在任意设备间无缝切换

由于所有连接均为出站，**无需端口转发、无需 VPN**，可穿透 NAT 和企业防火墙正常使用。

---

### 两种启动方式对比

| 命令 | 场景 | 是否保留对话历史 | 是否支持 flags |
|------|------|------|------|
| `claude rc` | 开启全新会话 | 否 | 是（如 `--sandbox`） |
| `/rc` | 在已有会话内激活 | 是 | 否 |

---

### 可用计划

- Pro、Max、Team、Enterprise 计划均支持
- API Key 用户**不支持**
- Team / Enterprise 需要管理员在 Claude Code 管理设置中先开启 Remote Control 开关

---

### 已知限制

- 网络断开约 10 分钟后会话超时（网络恢复后自动重连，但关机或崩溃会终止会话）
- 无法在移动端添加新的 MCP 服务器（需在启动前配置好）
- QR 码功能目前仅支持 macOS，Windows 暂不支持

---

### 实用建议

- 走开前用 `/rename` 给会话命名，方便在多会话列表中识别
- 用 `/rc` 而非 `claude rc` 保留已有对话上下文
- MCP 服务器和权限设置需在开启 Remote Control 前配置完毕

---

Sources:
- [Continue local sessions from any device with Remote Control - Claude Code Docs](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/remote-control)
- [Anthropic just released a mobile version of Claude Code called Remote Control | VentureBeat](https://venturebeat.com/orchestration/anthropic-just-released-a-mobile-version-of-claude-code-called-remote)
- [Claude Code Remote Control | Simon Willison](https://simonwillison.net/2026/Feb/25/claude-code-remote-control/)
- [Claude Code Changelog](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog)
- [GitHub Releases - anthropics/claude-code](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/releases)

该功能于 **2026 年 2 月**作为 Research Preview（研究预览版）推出，要求 **Claude Code v2.1.51 或更高版本**。

- 用 `/rc` 而非 `claude rc` 保留已有对话上下文
- MCP 服务器和权限设置需在开启 Remote Control 前配置完毕

---

Sources:
- [Continue local sessions from any device with Remote Control - Claude Code Docs](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/remote-control)
- [Anthropic just released a mobile version of Claude Code called Remote Control | VentureBeat](https://venturebeat.com/orchestration/anthropic-just-released-a-mobile-version-of-claude-code-called-remote)
- [Claude Code Remote Control | Simon Willison](https://simonwillison.net/2026/Feb/25/claude-code-remote-control/)
- [Claude Code Changelog](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog)
- [GitHub Releases - anthropics/claude-code](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/releases)

content.thinking: The user is asking about Remote Control feature support version for Claude Code. Let me provide them with the information from the search results.

content.text:
**Claude Code Remote Control** 功能要求 **v2.1.51 或更高版本**。

---

### 使用方式

| 命令 | 场景 | 说明 |
|------|------|------|
| `claude rc` | 新建会话并启用远程控制 | 可带 flags（如 `--sandbox`） |
| `/rc` | 当前会话启用远程控制 | 保留现有对话历史 |

启动后会生成一个会话 URL 和二维码，可在以下端同步使用：
- `claude.ai/code`（网页端）
- Claude iOS / Android 移动应用

### 注意事项

- 你的版本 **2.1.78** 已支持该功能
- 会话运行在本地机器上，所有 MCP 服务器和工具配置保持可用
- 网络断开约 10 分钟后会话超时
- Pro/Max/Team/Enterprise 计划支持，API Key 用户不支持

message.content: 我/后没有 rc 命令

content.thinking:
用户说他在使用 `/rc` 命令后没有看到 rc 命令。这可能意味着：
1. 他输入 `/rc` 后没有反应
2. 他输入 `/` 后没有看到 rc 选项

让我帮用户排查这个问题。首先我需要确认：
1. 他的 Claude Code 版本确实是 2.1.78
2. Remote Control 功能是否在他的账号计划中可用
3. 是否可能是权限或配置问题

由于用户说 "/后没有 rc 命令"，这听起来像是输入 `/` 后没有看到 `rc` 命令的自动补全选项。

让我询问更多细节来帮助排查。

content.text: 我来帮你排查这个问题。

content.thinking: 版本是 2.1.78，应该支持 remote control。让我询问用户更多细节来排查问题。

content.text:
你的版本 2.1.78 是支持 Remote Control 的。请确认以下几点：

### 1. 输入方式
直接输入 `/rc` 然后按 **回车**，不是等待自动补全菜单。Remote Control 命令可能不会显示在 `/` 的自动补全列表中。

### 2. 账号计划检查
Remote Control 仅支持以下计划：
- ✅ Pro / Max / Team / Enterprise
- ❌ API Key 用户不支持

如果你是用 API Key 登录的，需要切换到 Pro 账号：
```bash
claude logout
claude login  # 使用 Pro/Team 账号登录
```

### 3. 直接尝试命令
直接在终端输入（不在对话中）：
```bash
claude rc
```

### 4. 检查是否已启用
某些 Team/Enterprise 账号需要管理员在 [Claude Code 管理设置](https://console.anthropic.com/code) 中开启 Remote Control 开关。

---

请告诉我：
1. 你是用什么方式登录的（Pro/Team 账号还是 API Key）？
2. 输入 `/rc` 后终端有什么反应？是完全没反应，还是显示错误信息？

Archive a completed change in the experimental workflow.

**Input**: Optionally specify a change name after `/opsx:archive` (e.g., `/opsx:archive add-auth`). If omitted, check if it can be inferred from conversation context. If vague or ambiguous you MUST prompt for available changes.

**Steps**

1. **If no change name provided, prompt for selection**

   Run `openspec list --json` to get available changes. Use the **AskUserQuestion tool** to let the user select.

   Show only active changes (not already archived).
   Include the schema used for each change if available.

   **IMPORTANT**: Do NOT guess or auto-select a change. Always let the user choose.

2. **Check artifact completion status**

   Run `openspec status --change "<name>" --json` to check artifact completion.

   Parse the JSON to understand:
   - `schemaName`: The workflow being used
   - `artifacts`: List of artifacts with their status (`done` or other)

   **If any artifacts are not `done`:**
   - Display warning listing incomplete artifacts
   - Prompt user for confirmation to continue
   - Proceed if user confirms

3. **Check task completion status**

   Read the tasks file (typically `tasks.md`) to check for incomplete tasks.

   Count tasks marked with `- [ ]` (incomplete) vs `- [x]` (complete).

   **If incomplete tasks found:**
   - Display warning showing count of incomplete tasks
   - Prompt user for confirmation to continue
   - Proceed if user confirms

   **If no tasks file exists:** Proceed without task-related warning.

4. **Assess delta spec sync state**

   Check for delta specs at `openspec/changes/<name>/specs/`. If none exist, proceed without sync prompt.

   **If delta specs exist:**
   - Compare each delta spec with its corresponding main spec at `openspec/specs/<capability>/spec.md`
   - Determine what changes would be applied (adds, modifications, removals, renames)
   - Show a combined summary before prompting

   **Prompt options:**
   - If changes needed: "Sync now (recommended)", "Archive without syncing"
   - If already synced: "Archive now", "Sync anyway", "Cancel"

   If user chooses sync, use Task tool (subagent_type: "general-purpose", prompt: "Use Skill tool to invoke openspec-sync-specs for change '<name>'. Delta spec analysis: <include the analyzed delta spec summary>"). Proceed to archive regardless of choice.

5. **Perform the archive**

   Create the archive directory if it doesn't exist:
   ```bash
   mkdir -p openspec/changes/archive
   ```

   Generate target name using current date: `YYYY-MM-DD-<change-name>`

   **Check if target already exists:**
   - If yes: Fail with error, suggest renaming existing archive or using different date
   - If no: Move the change directory to archive

   ```bash
   mv openspec/changes/<name> openspec/changes/archive/YYYY-MM-DD-<name>
   ```

6. **Display summary**

   Show archive completion summary including:
   - Change name
   - Schema that was used
   - Archive location
   - Spec sync status (synced / sync skipped / no delta specs)
   - Note about any warnings (incomplete artifacts/tasks)

**Output On Success**

```
## Archive Complete

**Change:** <change-name>
**Schema:** <schema-name>
**Archived to:** openspec/changes/archive/YYYY-MM-DD-<name>/
**Specs:** ✓ Synced to main specs

All artifacts complete. All tasks complete.
```

**Output On Success (No Delta Specs)**

```
## Archive Complete

**Change:** <change-name>
**Schema:** <schema-name>
**Archived to:** openspec/changes/archive/YYYY-MM-DD-<name>/
**Specs:** No delta specs

All artifacts complete. All tasks complete.
```

**Output On Success With Warnings**

```
## Archive Complete (with warnings)

**Change:** <change-name>
**Schema:** <schema-name>
**Archived to:** openspec/changes/archive/YYYY-MM-DD-<name>/
**Specs:** Sync skipped (user chose to skip)

**Warnings:**
- Archived with 2 incomplete artifacts
- Archived with 3 incomplete tasks
- Delta spec sync was skipped (user chose to skip)

Review the archive if this was not intentional.
```

**Output On Error (Archive Exists)**

```
## Archive Failed

**Change:** <change-name>
**Target:** openspec/changes/archive/YYYY-MM-DD-<name>/

Target archive directory already exists.

**Options:**
1. Rename the existing archive
2. Delete the existing archive if it's a duplicate
3. Wait until a different date to archive
```

**Guardrails**
- Always prompt for change selection if not provided
- Use artifact graph (openspec status --json) for completion checking
- Don't block archive on warnings - just inform and confirm
- Preserve .openspec.yaml when moving to archive (it moves with the directory)
- Show clear summary of what happened
- If sync is requested, use the Skill tool to invoke `openspec-sync-specs` (agent-driven)
- If delta specs exist, always run the sync assessment and show the combined summary before prompting
